messages.content:
What process does typical mediation follow?
Group of answer choices

a) Typical mediation is an intermittent process loosely following six different steps

b) Typical mediation is a sequential process from opening statement to final agreement

c) Typical mediation follows a sequential process but may include loop-backs

d) Typical mediation is an informal process of ‘to-and-fro’ until an agreement is reached
c) Typical mediation follows a sequential process but may include loop-backs
c) Typical mediation follows a sequential process but may include loop-backs

Which one of the following cannot be regarded as an advantage of advisory arbitration awards?
Group of answer choices

a) The LRA prescribes the procedure to follow with advisory arbitration that aids in expediting the process

b) It provides parties with an independent neutral view of the merits of the dispute

c) A well-reasoned advisory award may convince a party whether or not to continue to arbitration

d) In the case of refusal to bargain, the advice may convince a party not to continue with industrial action if unlawful
a) The LRA prescribes the procedure to follow with advisory arbitration that aids in expediting the process

Sandy and Fred are divorcing and they work with a neutral third party that helps them resolve their divorce issues by providing an opinion on the best solution in dividing up assets and property. What is this an example of?
Group of answer choices

a) Arb-con

b) Facilitation

c) Con-opinion

d) Ombudsman
c) Con-opinion

At what stage does a grievance evolve into a dispute?
Group of answer choices

a) When the grievance is not addressed appropriately

b) The moment it is penned to paper

c) The moment the conflict is partially formalised

d) A conflict always turns into a dispute
c) The moment the conflict is partially formalised

A negotiator responds to a sensitive question by deliberately misinterpreting it. What technique is he employing?
Group of answer choices

a) Reframing

b) Derailing

c) Redirecting

d) Blocking
a) Reframing

In which of the following circumstances will it be desirable to enter into a negotiation?
Group of answer choices

a) When parties believe negotiation will get them the resolution they desire

b) When negotiating a bribe will ensure you get past a deadlock situation

c) When trying to stop a fight between two other people

d) When negotiation is not by choice but mandated by time constraints
a) When parties believe negotiation will get them the resolution they desire

Which one of the following ways of communication is thought to be effective?
Group of answer choices

a) Constantly interrupting to rephrase what the other party is saying, to show you are listening

b) Talk in a monotone voice, not changing your voice inflection or volume with a neutral facial expression to avoid bringing emotion to the negotiating table

c) Show confidence and speak as if your ideas are the only ideas worth consideration

d) Frame messages in such a way that it takes into account the frame of reference of the listener
d) Frame messages in such a way that it takes into account the frame of reference of the listener

Which one of the following ways of communicating would inhibit a negotiator’s chances of success?
Group of answer choices

a) Asking open-ended questions to buy time

b) Creating a facilitative negotiating environment

c) Showing emotions rather than describing them

d) Asking questions to apply pressure on the other side
d) Asking questions to apply pressure on the other side

n which one of the following examples can a power imbalance be problematic to the mediation process?
Group of answer choices

a) When one party is unable to speak for themselves

b) Any degree of power imbalance will be problematic

c) When a mediator fails to create equality between parties

d) When a mediator has weak persuasive powers
a) When one party is unable to speak for themselves

n which one of the following examples can a power imbalance be problematic to the mediation process?
Group of answer choices

a) When one party is unable to speak for themselves

b) Any degree of power imbalance will be problematic

c) When a mediator fails to create equality between parties

d) When a mediator has weak persuasive powers
b) Any degree of power imbalance will be problematic

What advantage does the fact that mediation is done on a ‘without prejudice’ basis hold for participants?
Group of answer choices

a) Relationship -saving

b) Unbiased procedure

c) Confidentiality

d) Self-determination
c) Confidentiality

Which one of the following statements is true of mediation?
Group of answer choices

a) In most instances, mediation by a neutral third party is an involuntary process

b) In many European societies, mediation is used as the primary method to resolve disputes

c) Mediation is used by parties that need help in resolving their dispute but still want to maintain control

d) With mediation, parties are obliged to agree to a solution proposed by the neutral third party
c) Mediation is used by parties that need help in resolving their dispute but still want to maintain control

What type of mediation requires the mediator to guide the parties through the process of reaching an agreement but does not advise the parties or attempt to influence their decisions? 
Group of answer choices

a) Evaluative mediation

b) Transformative mediation

c) Settlement mediation

d) Facilitative mediation
d) Facilitative mediation

In what way does the law impact mediation?
Group of answer choices

a) South African law provides for a duty to bargain in good faith

b) A court can review the conduct of parties in the negotiation process

c) South African law provides prescriptive mediation procedures

d) A settlement agreement must comply with general contractual requirements

b) A court can review the conduct of parties in the negotiation process
d) A settlement agreement must comply with general contractual requirements

a) South African law provides for a duty to bargain in good faith
b) A court can review the conduct of parties in the negotiation process
d) A settlement agreement must comply with general contractual requirements

What type of mediation is used by a tribal chief of the local community calling for a lekgotla to resolve problems within the village causing conflict? 
Group of answer choices

a) Evaluative mediation

b) Transformative mediation

c) Settlement mediation

d) Facilitative mediation
d) Facilitative mediation

Which one of the following falls outside the mediation process?
Group of answer choices

a) Preparation

b) Generating options

c) Opening statements

d) Appeal
d) Appeal

What is meant by the neutrality of the mediator?
Group of answer choices

a) The mediator is required to be a person of authority

b) The mediator is required to remain detached from the outcome

c) The mediator is required to assist and boost the weaker party

d) The mediator is required to be compassionate
b) The mediator is required to remain detached from the outcome

Unresolved disputes are the result of poor negotiation and problem-solving skills of the parties involved. What should the focus of negotiations be?
Group of answer choices

a) Identifying common ground and needs

b) Constructive communication

c) Empowerment of parties

d) Identifying information needed to settle
a) Identifying common ground and needs

Which one of the following is the primary role of the mediator?
Group of answer choices

a) Imposing pressure to accept proposed ideas

b) To offer a solution to the parties involved

c) To assist parties in reaching a settlement

d) Facilitating negotiation and problem solving
d) Facilitating negotiation and problem solving

Which one of the following is the primary role of the mediator?
Group of answer choices

a) Imposing pressure to accept proposed ideas

b) To offer a solution to the parties involved

c) To assist parties in reaching a settlement

d) Facilitating negotiation and problem solving
c) To assist parties in reaching a settlement
